requirement: Source foreign buyer activity and international capital flows into London prime residential

sources_used.source: Bank of England — UK International Investment Position (IIP) and Balance of Payments
sources_used.data: UK quarterly IIP: FDI inward stock (£m), FDI inward net equity flows (£m), current account % GDP, property/investment income credits and debits

sources_used.source: HMRC Monthly Property Transactions Commentary — GOV.UK
sources_used.file: data/sources/UK monthly property transactions commentary - GOV.UK.html
sources_used.data: UK residential and non-residential transaction volumes commentary
sources_used.result: COMMENTARY ONLY — no buyer-country data. UK transaction volumes only.

sources_used.data: UK residential and non-residential transaction counts: monthly (April 2005 – March 2026), quarterly, and annual by country (England/Scotland/Wales/NI/UK)
sources_used.result: SUCCESS — transaction volumes. No buyer-country segmentation.

key_findings.fdi_inward_stock: UK accumulated foreign investment grew from ~£500bn (2000) to ~£2 trillion (2025) — 4x increase over 25 years.
key_findings.fdi_net_flows: Annual net FDI equity inflows averaged £8-43bn 2016-2023. 2024 saw near-zero net FDI (£-628m avg) — lowest in series. 2025 recovered to +£6,958m avg.
key_findings.current_account_deficit: UK runs -0.7% to -5.3% CA deficit. Widest: 2016 (-5.3%, post-Brexit vote). Current: -2.5% (2025). A wider deficit with weak FDI = structural GBP pressure = better effective discount for USD buyers.
key_findings.property_income_deficit: UK consistently pays out more in property/investment income to foreign investors than it earns abroad. PI debits exceed credits by ~£20-60bn annually — foreign ownership of UK assets generates substantial income outflows.
key_findings.london_signal: Weak UK FDI flows 2024-25 = reduced international capital appetite = headwind for prime London prices. Strong FDI flows 2017-18 (post-Brexit resolution) and 2021-23 (post-COVID GBP weakness) coincide with London outperformance periods.

what_is_available.international_capital_flows: UK FDI inward flows and stock — proxy for international investment appetite into UK assets including London
what_is_available.current_account: UK external deficit — indicates GBP structural pressure; wider deficit + weak FDI = better USD buyer discount
what_is_available.property_income: UK PI income deficit — foreign ownership of UK assets generates income outflows

what_is_still_missing.london_specific_fdi: BoE IIP is UK-level only. London-specific FDI is not published by BoE.
what_is_still_missing.buyer_country_pct: % of London transactions by foreign nationality (HK, UAE, US, CH, CN, EU). Requires HMRC buyer-country data.
what_is_still_missing.non_resident_sdtl: Time series of non-resident SDLT surcharge transactions and revenue. Would show impact of 2021 surcharge on foreign buyer volumes.

dat_183_assessment.recommended_next_step: User to download HMRC 'non-resident SDLT transactions' ODS from gov.uk statistics page. File would contain: transactions by non-resident status × quarter × region.
